title: DSA: Sorting Algorithms — Bubble, Merge, Quick Sort
content:
### Day 32: DSA: Sorting Algorithms — Bubble, Merge, Quick Sort

**Bubble Sort** — O(n²): Compare adjacent, swap if needed. Simple but slow.

**Merge Sort** — O(n log n): Divide in half, sort each, merge. Stable and reliable.

**Quick Sort** — O(n log n) avg: Pick pivot, partition, recurse. Fast in practice.
practiceQuestions: Why is merge sort O(n log n)?
keyTakeaways: Bubble Sort — O(n²): Compare adjacent, swap if needed.
Merge Sort — O(n log n): Divide in half, sort each, merge.
Quick Sort — O(n log n) avg: Pick pivot, partition, recurse.
